title: React State Quiz
description: Evaluate your understanding of state, re-rendering, and the differences between state and props.

questions.question: Which and/or what is the best way to define React state?
options: Data that is passed down to a component (like arguments).
Data that is managed within a component and can change over time.
A global variable accessible by every component.
None of the above.

questions.question: Which of the following is true about state in React?
options: State is public and can be modified by any component.
State is private to the component it is defined in.
State is only for class components.
State is immutable and cannot be changed.

questions.question: What happens in a React component when its state changes?
options: Nothing happens until the user reloads the page.
The component is automatically re-rendered to reflect the new state in the UI.
The component is deleted and recreated.
React logs a warning to the console.

questions.question: Which is a key difference between state and props?
options: Props are mutable, while state is immutable.
State is managed within a component, while props are passed to a component.
State is passed from a child to a parent.
There is no difference.

questions.question: Can a parent component access a child component's local state directly?
options: Yes, using the 'state' prop.
No, state is private to the component it belongs to.
Yes, but only if they are in the same file.
Only if the child is a class component.
